_comment: Per-section TTS plan. Sections are spoken sequentially; the scenario_anchor maps to a Sequence in TestVideo.tsx so the audio plays during that beat. Owner sections (A, I, K) overlay the B-roll clips; VO sections overlay the screen captures.

sections.speaker: vo
sections.text: Independent bakeries throw out 30 to 40 percent of what they make. The waste is uncertainty, not ignorance.

sections.speaker: vo
sections.text: Sign in with the bakery's tenant slug, email, and password. The interesting part is what comes next.

sections.speaker: vo
sections.text: Sales history loaded — per SKU, per branch, per day. The forecaster trains on this.

sections.speaker: vo
sections.text: LightGBM trees, seven quantile heads. Pure TypeScript inside a Cloudflare Worker.

sections.speaker: vo
sections.text: Today's bake plan — newsvendor-picked from the forecast. Switch branches: same model, different demand shape.

sections.speaker: vo
sections.text: Open a SKU. The quantile band shows where demand is likely to land; the driver bars show why.

sections.speaker: vo
sections.text: Click Ask Gemma why. The question is prefilled and sent to Gemma 4. It calls the forecaster, reads the top drivers, and grounds the answer in the numbers.

sections.speaker: vo
sections.text: At end of day, one photo is enough. Gemma 4 counts what is left.

sections.speaker: vo
sections.text: Gemma 4 counts the display case and suggests markdowns.

sections.speaker: vo
sections.text: Sign out. The session ends, the tokens revoke, and the page returns to sign-in.

sections.speaker: vo
sections.text: Within two months, the model learns the bakery better than the baker remembers it. Validated against nine forecasting benchmarks — the architecture transfers across foundation models.
